title: Planet Manifest Schema
description: Validates the manifest.json file for a CyberHeroes planet or moon module. The manifest is the authoritative index describing a planet's metadata, theme, intro story content, and the ordered sequence of curriculum parts.

intro.computer_text: Hello Cyber Hero! We need your help...

transition.message: Use what you learned to defeat the enemy!

slug.description: Kebab-case unique identifier for this planet. Must exactly match the directory name under src/data/planets/. Example: 'privacy-planet'.

name.description: Short human-readable planet name shown in navigation menus and headers. Example: 'Privacy Planet'.

title.description: Full display title for the lesson module shown on the planet's intro and table of contents screens. Example: 'Sensitive Information'.

order.description: Curriculum sequence position. Planets and moons are displayed in ascending order. Must be a positive integer starting at 1.

active.description: Whether this planet is published and accessible to students. Set to false for planned but not-yet-released planets.

parent_planet_slug.description: For companion moons only: the slug of the parent planet this moon extends. Omit this field entirely for primary planets. A moon with this field set is treated as a sequel/companion to the referenced planet.

theme.description: Color token map defining the visual palette for rendering this planet's UI. All four color fields are required.

primary.description: Primary brand color used for headings, buttons, and key UI elements. Accepts any valid CSS color value (hex, rgb, named color).

secondary.description: Secondary color used for supporting UI elements, borders, and hover states.

background.description: Page background color applied to the planet's lesson and quiz screens.

accent.description: Highlight and call-to-action color used for correct answer indicators, progress bars, and interactive elements.

icon.description: Filename or path to the planet icon asset, relative to the planet's asset directory. Used in the planet selection screen and navigation.

intro.description: Story intro content shown on the mission briefing screen before the lesson begins. Contains the narrative setup and mission computer text.

intro_text.description: Narrative intro paragraph shown on the mission briefing screen, setting the story context for the planet's lesson. Supports newline characters (\n).
examples: Welcome to Privacy Planet. Life here is generally peaceful, and people usually have nothing much to worry about.

computer_image_name.description: Filename of the image displayed in the mission computer panel on the briefing screen, relative to the shared assets directory.

computer_text.description: Text displayed alongside the computer image in the mission briefing. Provides the specific mission objective in the voice of mission control.
examples: Hello Cyber Hero! We are told that Patrick the Predatorus is keeping something very valuable that he stole.

parts.description: Ordered array of curriculum parts that make up this planet's lesson sequence. Parts alternate between lesson content (with lesson_page_range) and quiz content (with quiz_slugs). Must contain at least one part.

items.description: A single curriculum part, which is either a lesson section or a quiz section. Lesson parts have lesson_page_range; quiz parts have quiz_slugs. A part must have at least one of these two fields.

anyOf.description: A lesson part must include lesson_page_range.
A quiz part must include quiz_slugs.

id.description: Kebab-case identifier for this part, unique within the planet. Convention: 'part-1', 'part-1-quiz', 'part-2', 'part-2-quiz', etc.

title.description: Human-readable display title for this curriculum part, shown in the table of contents.

part_style.description: CSS style class applied to this part's table of contents entry. Convention: 'lesson-N' for lesson parts and 'quiz-N' for quiz parts, where N is the part number.

lesson_page_range.description: Planet-local page range for this lesson section. Present only on lesson parts (not quiz parts). Page numbers are always planet-local: they restart at 1 for each planet regardless of curriculum position.

start.description: First planet-local page number of this lesson section. Must be a positive integer. The first lesson part of any planet must have start: 1.

end.description: Last planet-local page number of this lesson section. Must be greater than or equal to start.
end must be greater than or equal to start. Validated at runtime since JSON Schema draft-07 cannot cross-reference sibling properties in a constraint.

quiz_slugs.description: Ordered list of quiz slug strings belonging to this quiz part. Each slug must match the filename (without .json) of a file under this planet's quizzes/ directory. Multiple slugs allow a part to contain several quiz types presented in sequence.

items.description: A kebab-case quiz slug matching a file at quizzes/<slug>.json within this planet's directory.

transition.description: Optional story transition shown to the student just before the quiz begins. Present on quiz parts only. Contains the character who speaks and the motivational message.

character.description: Display name of the character delivering the transition message. Must match a character name used in the planet's lesson pages.

message.description: Story message shown to the student to set up the quiz narrative. Supports newline characters (\n).
examples: Use what you learned to Defeat Enemy 1 and help me get back my information!!!
Huh looks like an enemy is trying to follow us! Quick let's defeat them with our knowledge!
